reviews.review_description:
Downloaded the Free Trial last night and went through the tutorial and then a brief look at the respiratory area and then, time ran out.

Now I have purchased the full simulator, my first impressions are; wow, the amount of work that has been put into this for such a small cost is amazing. I get it though. Keep the price low and hopefully more will buy it for their studies.

For me personally, I work for the NHS Ambulance Service and I will find this software very beneficial.
Books are great but you cannot remove parts or get inside areas, unlike this software - hence my title “Innerspace”.

A suggestion for future updates if possible. The ability to go into the actual operating theatre and work on the body that is lying on the slab/table.

A great addition for anyone studying anatomy for professional or just personal use. I look forward to delving deeper.

developer_response.body:
Thank you for the thoughtful review, and we’re glad you’re enjoying our app!

It’s great to hear it is useful in your work with the NHS Ambulance Service, and we love the “Innerspace” comparison. Your suggestion about an operating theatre style environment is noted and it’s exactly the kind of feedback that helps guide future updates.

Thanks again for the support, and enjoy exploring Human Anatomy VR further.

reviews.review_description:
Hello,

I am a massage therapist and I bought this app mainly to study anatomy for my work. However, I cannot find any way to stop the model from automatically rotating. The constant spinning makes it quite difficult to examine the anatomy carefully.
I previously used the PS4/PSVR version of this app, and the model did not rotate automatically there, which made it much easier to study.
Is there a way to disable the auto-rotation of the model? If not, could this be added as an option in a future update?
This issue is unfortunately quite distracting, and I may consider requesting a refund if there is no way to stop the rotation.
Thank you.

developer_response.body:
Thank you for your feedback and for using Human Anatomy VR for your anatomy studies & work.

Just to make sure we understand the issue correctly, do you mean the auto-rotation within the quiz feature, or is the model rotating on its own during normal study mode as well?

If the model is rotating on its own while you are simply studying, please try removing the battery from the left controller for a moment and check whether it still happens. This could help us identify whether the issue is related to controller input.

Please also contact us at info@medicinevirtual.com and we will be happy to help you directly.

reviews.review_description: I love what the app is capable of doing. But one glaring thing missing (unless I'm blind) is a reset button. When you manipulate the model a lot and want to reset it and your current settings, I don't see a button that allows me to do that.

developer_response.body:
Mr. Vosough, thank you for your feedback.
To reset the model’s position and orientation, use the B button on your right-hand Quest controller. Press B to undo your last action, or hold B to reset everything back to the default model position.

I hope this helps!

reviews.review_description: I purchased unintentionally

developer_response.body: Hi Denis, thank you for your review. If you purchased Human Anatomy VR unintentionally, please contact the Meta Horizon support team through the following page: https://www.meta.com/en-gb/help/quest/ They will be able to assist you with cancelling your store purchase.

reviews.review_title: Pretty useful, but still missing a lot
reviews.review_description:
This app is pretty good for most gross anatomy, but it really falls short when it comes to smaller and microscopic anatomy.
A few things that are missing in it, and these are not all of them:
1. No detailed heart organ option (This is one of the most important organs in the body, and I can't view it in detail like things such as the sarcomere, which isn't in much detail, but at least it's an option to view by itself).
2. Speaking of the sarcomere, it doesn't go into detail at all. You can't break it down to active and myosin fibers, view t-tubules, or anything like that. You can't break it down past the gross structure of the sarcomere really. 
3. I'm not sure what it's like on other parts of the app, but when I was doing bones, there were many bone features that were named, but the description box said "no description" or something like that.

Ultimately, I don't think the app is that great a value with how much ISN'T in it. For college-level A&P, I'd say this is useful for less than half of the material. There are many other free options online that go into more detail.

developer_response.body: Dear Recognizer_1, thank you for your thoughtful feedback. This version of Human Anatomy VR is our consumer edition, which focuses mainly on gross anatomy and general learning. It is not designed or licensed for college-level A&P, so some of the advanced structures you mentioned are not included here. For higher-education use, we offer Human Anatomy VR for Institutions, which provides a fully detailed heart model with a beating-heart animation, more extensive microanatomy, and over 4,500 bony features with complete descriptions. This educational edition was created and accredited specifically for high-school and university programs and offers significantly more depth than the consumer version. We appreciate you taking the time to share your experience and hope this helps clarify the difference between both editions.

reviews.review_title: A deeper understanding of the human body
reviews.review_description: A great application for learning human anatomy, with a pleasant interface, intuitive controls, structure descriptions, excellent animations and detailed visuals. I also tried the institutional version, which additionally includes local translations of anatomical terms. It was incredibly helpful for my anatomy studies. The children were enthusiastic too—they tried the quizzes and for a deeper understanding of the human body, it was invaluable.

developer_response.body:
Thank you for your wonderful review! We’re glad Human Anatomy VR supported your studies, and great to hear the kids enjoyed our quizzes too. We appreciate your feedback!
Team Virtual Medicine

reviews.review_title: Make limb poses match my avatar movement
reviews.review_description: Hey can you make it so that when I move my own hands, the 3D model moves accordingly? And map my arms and finger motion to feet if I am analyzing legs and feet? Also quest has head movement and pose estimation - would be amazing to position the models using these methods.

developer_response.body:
Hi nobydy,

Thank you for your positive review and for sharing your suggestions. We appreciate your input and will be taking your ideas into consideration as we plan our future development roadmap.

Your feedback helps us continue improving the experience, and we’re excited about exploring ways to make our models even more interactive.

Thanks again for helping us shape the future of the app!

reviews.review_description:
It has been useful to study for anatomy exams. One thing that I’m not sure how to do is to create custom Quizzes, as often I do not get asked for “the entire musculoskeletal system” but rather for just the major muscles and bones.

Would give it 5 stars if I could create my own quizzes and if I could change the color or contrast of the muscles and bones (medical cadavers often have a darker color that makes things harder to differentiate in real life).

developer_response.body:
Thank you for your feedback! We're glad to hear that Human Anatomy VR has been helpful for your anatomy studies.

We appreciate your suggestions regarding custom quiz creation and adjusting muscle and bone colors for better contrast. These are great ideas, and we will explore ways to integrate them into future updates to enhance the application.

reviews.review_description: I'm an RN.  Anytime I've got a patient with a condition I don't really understand, this app helps make every much easier to understand.  The only thing I don't like is that I didn't have this when I was in school years ago.  This would have been a huge help.

developer_response.body:
Thank you so much for your kind words! We're thrilled to hear our app is making a difference in your work as a Registered Nurse. 😊
Team Virtual Medicine

reviews.review_description: As a student of anatomy I frickin love this. The tutorial was quick and I learned everything I needed. The layers of many organs are extra cool.

developer_response.body: Thank you so much for your fantastic feedback! We're thrilled to hear you're loving the app and finding it helpful for your studies. —happy exploring! 😊

reviews.review_description: I used this app to prepare for my Medical School admission exams, and now I rely on it even more as anatomy class gets tougher. I definitely recommend it to all aspiring medical students! 😎

developer_response.body: Thank you so much for your kind words! We're happy to hear that the app has been helpful to you. Best of luck in your medical journey, and we appreciate your recommendation!

reviews.review_description: I bought this for PA school as it is far cheaper than a lot of other programs and is not subscription-based. There are so many features/functions, and the anatomy is spot on. There are very few things that this app cannot do. This is geared more towards college and graduate level of education, but works for any level. Thank you, devs, for making something so exceptional for such a low price.

developer_response.body:
Thank you so much for your wonderful feedback! We’re thrilled to hear that you’re finding our app helpful in your PA School.
We’re committed to perfecting the app and providing even more value to our users. If you ever have any suggestions or feedback, feel free to reach out.

reviews.review_title: I need to use the Dicom function
reviews.review_description: Hopefully the personal version will be able to get the dicom feature with an update, or I can unlock the feature with a single payment. After all, I'm a person, not an institution, but I want to use dicom.

developer_response.body:
Dear xbj970307,

Thank you for your feedback. The advanced VR DICOM feature is not included in the personal version of our application in order to keep the app affordable for everyone interested in learning anatomy. To access an advanced feature such as VR DICOM, you'll need to purchase the Institutional Version, which offers these advanced features and can also be purchased by individuals.

If you're not satisfied with the personal version of our app, you can request a refund through Meta’s refund policy: https://www.meta.com/legal/quest/quest-rift-content-refund-policy/

For more information or to acquire the VR DICOM feature, please contact us at: info@medicinevirtual.com.

Best regards,
Team Virtual Medicine

reviews.review_description:
This application is brilliant, I'm having a blast on it, separating the different parts and observing everything, plus there's lots of detail, great 3D models, the option of becoming small, quizzes, etc. The MR mode is really well done and the hand tracking works really well. And the animations are incredible. But I would have liked a few more things:
- For ant mode, it would be nice to be able to switch to a larger size or back to a smaller size without the ‘hardware’ turning over in its place.
- It would also be nice to be able to separate more parts of the body, for example, I take the cerebellum or the pituitary gland and I can open it in two to see what's inside with the different parts (if there are any), and details on these components. Also for bones, muscles etc. In short, to have a bit more detail and 3D models.
- In-depth, detailed 3D models of the body's main organs would be very welcome.
- As for the quizzes, a correction for mistakes would not go amiss.
- Finally, when you take a part of the body and it has several layers, it would be really nice to be able to separate them so that you can take a closer look.

That's all, if you add this, the application will be perfect.

developer_response.body:
Thank you so much for your fantastic feedback! We’re thrilled you’re enjoying Human Anatomy VR and appreciate your detailed suggestions.

Your ideas about improving the ant mode, enhancing the ability to explore body parts in detail, and adding more robust quiz features are incredibly valuable. We’ll consider these enhancements as we continue to develop the app.

Thanks again for your insights and happy exploring!

reviews.review_title: I would change to 5 stars, but...
reviews.review_description:
The software is pretty good. But I have three complaints. 

1. I cannot quit the application. Once I press the oculus button to close the software, the whole thing freezes. And I have to force shutdown the quest 2 (sometimes that doesn't even work).

2. Please write the different sutura's (dont know the word in english) , sutura coronalis etc. Best would be to have sutures on one of the pluses under skeleton.

3. Please give us an option to rotate body parts by holding the grip button and using the left stick as an example. 

4. PLEASE let me rotate and manipulate the whole head. I seem to only be able to move the head without the mandibula. I want to look at the whole skull from under the skull, without using the ant-mode function.

1. Is the biggest reason for the low rating. However 3 and 4 are mightily annoying as well..

developer_response.body: Thank you for your feedback. We appreciate you bringing these issues to our attention and understand your frustration. Please know that we are constantly working on perfecting the software and your suggestions will help guide future updates.

reviews.review_description:
Update********

I tried the MR mode. Now this app if perfect for me.  Thank you for letting me know it was implemented in the app.  

ORIGINAL REVIEW ******


I purchase my Q3 for games and experiences such as table tennis and  Star Wars.  

I took a chance on this educational app on "Try  before you buy."

I am hooked.  I purchased it with no regrets.

Learning about the human body in stand alone VR is awesome.

This is a pleasant break from games.  

I strongly recommend this INVESTMENT for anyone.

One recommendation is a MR option.

Great job developers.  VR needs this kind of educational, while still fun, type of app.

developer_response.body:
Thank you for your wonderful review! We’re thrilled that you’re enjoying the MR mode and that the app has become a perfect fit for you.

Thanks again for your support and happy learning!

reviews.review_description: The video introduction in the Meta Store states that Human Anatomy VR can display DICOM files. I purchased it explicitly for that purpose, but this version will not display any DICOM MRI or CT scan images: to view DICOM files, I would have to pay $500/year for another version of the app. It is a dishonest bait-and-switch, so I'm requesting a refund, even though the rest of the app seems good.

developer_response.body:
Hello climate-change , we apologize for any misunderstanding and appreciate the opportunity to clarify the situation.

In our introductory video available on the Meta Store, we have included a specific warning message (”This feature is only available for a professional and institutional license”) to clarify that the ability to display DICOM files is a premium feature, available only to users with an professional and institutional license. We regret if this crucial information was not observed before your purchase.

We strive to make our application's capabilities transparent to all users. To ensure everyone can fully explore the features, we offer a trial of the advanced functionality, including DICOM file viewing, through our Human Anatomy VR for Institutions (Virtual Medicine Store). This trial is designed to help users evaluate the advanced features before deciding on any upgrade.

We appreciate that you enjoy other aspects of the app, and we're committed to ensuring a positive experience for all our users. If you have any more questions or require further assistance, please feel free to contact us.

We hope that after this explanation you will adjust your rating according to how you perceive the quality of our product. 
Thank you for your understanding and for considering Virtual Medicine for your educational needs.

Sincerely,
Virtual Medicine Team

reviews.review_description:
Very well indeed!
As someone who has always been fascinated by human anatomy, I am thrilled by app's intuitive, easy-to-use interface and features like Ant mode and passthrough, which offer fantastic and detailed new perspectives. 
The quality of the 3D models, animations, and detailed descriptions was way better than I expected and overall enhanced my understanding and visualization of the complex body structures. But bottom line it is really fun just exploring the human body like this, especially when you can precisely modify what you want to see.
I look forward to time, when VR will be more widespread technology, because this app is a game changer in anatomy education for every student and enthusiast.

developer_response.body:
Thank you for your fantastic feedback! We’re thrilled to hear that you’re enjoying the app’s features like Ant and Passthrough modes, and that the quality of the 3D models has exceeded your expectations.

Thanks for being a part of our journey!

reviews.review_description: more languages please, when will the surgery simulations be available??

developer_response.body: Thank you for your feedback! We’re excited to share that we are adding new languages very soon, and we are also working towards adding the surgery simulations in the future.

reviews.review_description:
Genuinely the sole reason why I got a Quest. Hands down, this app dominates with not only 3D visuals and top-notch educational content, but also with friendly and intuitive UI,which makes it the ultimate standout tool for thorough and better understanding of the human body. 
All the best to you guys,the future is now 😎

developer_response.body:
Wow, thank you for such enthusiastic praise! We’re delighted to know that our app played a key role in your decision to get a Quest. Your appreciation for our 3D visuals and user-friendly design means a lot to us.

Here’s to exploring the future of anatomy education together! 😎

reviews.review_description: After my knee surgery, the Human Anatomy VR app was incredibly useful. It clarified a lot for me, giving me a brand-new perspective on my body and allowing me to visualize exactly what the surgeons worked on.

developer_response.body:
Thank you for your wonderful feedback! We're thrilled to hear that our app was useful during your recovery from knee surgery. It’s rewarding to know that it helped you understand and visualize your body better.

Wishing you all the best on your healing journey!

reviews.review_description: The Human Anatomy VR app is an amazing tool for every athlete and anyone interested in how their body works. It offers a detailed and interactive view of the human body that is invaluable for understanding and improving fitness and health.

developer_response.body: We are grateful for your positive feedback! Our mission is to make anatomy accessible and engaging not only for medical students, but for everyone. Keep exploring and enjoy the fitness aspects of the app.

reviews.review_description: Diving into the Human Anatomy VR app is like stepping into a futuristic anatomy lab - a must-have for any med student’s virtual toolkit!

developer_response.body: We appreciate your kind words! It’s great to hear that you also see Human Anatomy VR as a necessary addition to any med student’s toolkit. We’re committed to providing the most realistic and innovative learning experience!

reviews.review_description: Best anatomy app :) very intuitive and eazy to use. Models are in great detail and with the features in handmenu the experience with this app is very smooth!

developer_response.body: We are thrilled that you rate our app as the best in anatomy! It's fantastic that you find it intuitive, we've put a lot of effort into ensuring a seamless experience. Thank you for your positive feedback and we wish you an enjoyable anatomical exploration!

reviews.review_description:
This app is really great for learning anatomy! Way better than many non vr apps i know for pc and ipad offering high quality models and even a very unique ant mode with which one can go through canals and check where some small canals of the human body lead to. I also really appreciate the function that enables the user to look things up on the internet and open and pin a webpage in place. I can highly recommend it to med students, medical professionals and institutes and will definitely try to convince my university, which is currently modernizing the anatomy department, to consider getting this. It is absolutely worth the money but there are still some important functions missing that I would like to point out:

- being able to grab, size and move the whole model without taking it apart by accident
- locking the model in place, especially in the pass through mode
- the model moves upwards (even almost going through the ceiling) when it is enlargened which is very very impractical. I cant view the model at all when it is meters above my head and have to use ant mode to fly there. This shouldnt be the case and I should be able to take the model and bring it down without having to do so each part seperately.
- finish the female model, so far only the male model is complete and the female model only offers to show the main differences. As a medical student I want to be able to look at the differences and also see their relations to different anatomical items which would require a complete anatomical model.
- The medical descriptions are ok at best. I understand that this is an atlas and not a medical book but it would be grest if we could have better descriptions and infos of the items.

Further ideas for the app:
It would be awesome to see this app being taken further than a regular atlas as vr has huge potential so it would be cool to see maybe an embryo that one can look at in different stages in the future. An addition of radiological imagery, maybe 3d histology, 3d cross sections or a sliceable model would be really cool as well and I would definitely pay a hundred euros for a vr app that offers these features. 

4/5 Stars for now, 5/5 when the first 3 requests are fixed.

developer_response.body:
Thank you so much for your detailed and constructive feedback! We truly appreciate your positive comments and your valuable suggestions for improvement. 

- In case of scaling the whole model (without taking it apart) you can use the Scale Up/Down function in Tools, category Interactions. 
-  In case the scaled model interferes with the environment, please turn off the environment in Tools, category Applications, using the Dark Scene function or rotate the model to horizontal position using the Rotate model function in Interactions category. 
- We're excited to inform you that we have plans to upgrade the female model soon. As this is complex and time-consuming work, we thank you for your patience.
We're committed to enhancing the Human Anatomy VR app to meet your expectations. Your input will certainly help us make the app even better. Stay tuned for updates, and we hope to earn that 5/5 rating from you in the near future!

reviews.review_description:
Any plans for the female model to be completed?

Is there a way to remap the controls?

developer_response.body: Thank you for your interest in the Human Anatomy VR app! We are happy to share that the female model is complete and available for detailed observation! We are constantly collecting feedback from our customers and will include your idea for remapping the controls in the development timeline. Stay tuned for these exciting enhancements in our next releases!
